questions.question: Explain the difference between regular pointers and pointers to class members in C++. Give an example of each.
keyPoints: Regular pointers store addresses of independent variables/functions
Pointers to class members require class qualification in type
Regular pointers use * operator directly, member pointers use .* or ->*
Example: int* regPtr = &x; vs int Class::*mptr = &Class::member;

questions.question: Write a C++ program demonstrating pointer to a data member. Create a class with two integer members and access them using pointer-to-member.
keyPoints: Define class with data members
Declare pointer to member using ::* syntax
Initialize with &ClassName::member
Use object.*ptr to access member
Assign values and display output

questions.question: What are the operators .* and ->* used for? Explain with syntax and examples.
keyPoints: .* is used with objects: obj.*ptr
->* is used with object pointers: objPtr->*ptr
Both dereference pointer-to-member through an object
Syntax requires parentheses around expression

questions.question: Explain how pointers to static members differ from pointers to non-static members.
keyPoints: Static members don't belong to objects
Pointers to static use regular pointer syntax
No object required to access static via pointer
Example: int* ptr = &MyClass::staticVar;

questions.question: What is the purpose of typedef with pointer-to-members? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Simplifies complex member pointer declarations
Creates readable type aliases
Example: typedef int (MyClass::*IntMemFn)();
Makes function pointers easier to use

questions.question: Can pointers to class members access private members? Justify your answer.
keyPoints: Yes, from within class scope
Member pointers can point to private members
Dereferencing works within accessible scope
Useful for implementing callbacks and patterns

questions.question: Write a complete C++ program that demonstrates pointer to member function. Include a class with multiple functions and show how to call them through pointers.
keyPoints: Define class with at least 2-3 member functions
Declare pointer to member function with correct syntax
Initialize with &ClassName::functionName
Create object and call via object.*ptr()
Handle multiple functions and parameters
Show output demonstrating correct execution

questions.question: Explain the concept of pointers to class members in detail. Discuss data member pointers vs function member pointers, syntax, operators, and practical applications.
keyPoints: Definition and need for member pointers
Difference from regular pointers
Data member pointer syntax and usage
Function member pointer syntax and usage
Operators .* and ->* explanation
Static member special case
Practical applications - callbacks, patterns

questions.question: Design a menu-driven program using pointers to member functions to call different functions based on user choice. Implement a Calculator class with add, subtract, multiply, divide operations.
keyPoints: Create Calculator class with arithmetic functions
Array of function pointers or map
Use switch-case with member pointers
Handle user input and display results
Proper error handling for division by zero

questions.question: What are the complexities and considerations when using pointers to virtual member functions? Explain with examples.
keyPoints: Virtual function pointers work with polymorphism
Runtime resolution through vtable
Pointers to virtual can be overridden in derived
Dynamic binding behavior
Comparison with non-virtual function pointers
